message.content:
You are implementing one commit in a Rust project at /home/wink/data/prgs/rust/vc-x1. Change what `init` writes into a generated `.vc-config.toml` so it documents the settable-key surface. DO NOT commit, DO NOT push, DO NOT change version numbers or notes files, and DO NOT modify the repo's own committed `.vc-config.toml` or `.claude/.vc-config.toml`. Report what you changed.

# Context
`src/config_schema.rs` has `pub fn schema() -> &'static [ConfigKey]` with:
```rust
pub enum Home { User, WorkspaceCode, WorkspaceBot }
pub enum ValueKind { Str, Usize, ItemList }
pub struct ConfigKey { pub path: &'static str, pub homes: &'static [Home], pub kind: ValueKind,
                       pub default: Option<&'static str>, pub required: bool, pub dynamic: bool, pub doc: &'static str }
```

`src/init.rs` currently writes three hardcoded raw-string constants:
- `VC_CONFIG_CODE` (line ~294): header comment + `[workspace]\npath = "/"\nother-repo = ".claude"\n`
- `VC_CONFIG_SESSION` (line ~305): header + `[workspace]\npath = "/.claude"\nother-repo = ".."\n`
- `VC_CONFIG_APP_ONLY` (line ~331): header + `[workspace]\npath = "/"\n` (NO other-repo — its absence signals single-repo)

Written by `write_code_config` (line ~379), `write_session_config` (~387), `write_por_vc_config` (~355). The constants are used ONLY in `src/init.rs` and `src/init/tests.rs` (grep to confirm).

`src/init/tests.rs` asserts (lines ~78-85, ~859-860):
- `VC_CONFIG_CODE.contains("path = \"/\"")`, `.contains("other-repo = \".claude\"")`
- `VC_CONFIG_SESSION.contains("path = \"/.claude\"")`, `.contains("other-repo = \"..\"")`
- `VC_CONFIG_APP_ONLY.contains("path = \"/\"")`, `!VC_CONFIG_APP_ONLY.contains("other-repo")`

# Goal
Replace the three constants with a generator `render_vc_config(role) -> String` that emits, per role:
1. The SAME header comment + active `[workspace]` block as today (exact values — see above; keep the existing per-role header prose, including that the AppOnly/POR variant has NO `other-repo`).
2. THEN a commented block of the OTHER settable workspace-config keys, derived from `schema()`, so a new repo's config documents the full surface without pinning anything.

## The generator
```rust
/// Which repo a generated `.vc-config.toml` targets.
#[derive(Clone, Copy)]
enum ConfigRole { Code, Session, AppOnly }

fn render_vc_config(role: ConfigRole) -> String
```
- Start with the role's existing header comment + active `[workspace]` block (path always; `other-repo` only for Code=".claude" / Session=".."; AppOnly omits other-repo entirely — do NOT emit it even commented).
- Then append ONE commented optional-keys block, IDENTICAL across all three roles, rendered from `schema()`:
  - Select keys where `homes` contains `Home::WorkspaceCode` or `Home::WorkspaceBot`, AND whose `path` does NOT start with `"workspace."` (the `[workspace]` section is the active block above). That yields the `push.*` and `bot-session.*` keys — all optional, all with defaults.
  - Precede the block with a short comment, e.g.:
    ```
    # Optional keys — uncomment and edit to override the built-in
    # default. `vc-x1 config` prints this list from the binary.
    ```
  - Group by TOML section: split each key's `path` on its LAST `.` into `(section, leaf)`, emit each `[section]` header once (schema/first-seen order), then `# <leaf> = <value>   # <doc>` for each key.
  - `<value>`: `ValueKind::Usize` → bare (`68`); `Str`/`ItemList` → quoted (`"push-state.toml"`, `"headers,user,assistant,tool,summary"`). (All these keys have `Some(default)`.)
- Change `write_code_config`/`write_session_config`/`write_por_vc_config` to write `render_vc_config(ConfigRole::Code|Session|AppOnly)` instead of the constants. Remove the three now-dead constants (they're local; grep confirmed).

Keep the generator and `ConfigRole` `pub(crate)` if the tests need them; otherwise private is fine.

## CRITICAL correctness constraint
The commented keys MUST be real TOML comments (each line starts with `#`), so that loading the generated file with `crate::toml_simple::toml_load` yields ONLY the active `[workspace]` keys — `workspace.path` (and `workspace.other-repo` for dual roles) — and NONE of the commented keys. Verify this in a test (see below). The active `[workspace]` block must remain parseable exactly as before so `find_workspace_root` and the other readers keep working.

## Tests (src/init/tests.rs)
Update the existing 6 assertions to call `render_vc_config(...)` instead of the removed constants (e.g. `let code = render_vc_config(ConfigRole::Code); assert!(code.contains("path = \"/\""));`). Keep the POR `!contains("other-repo")` assertion. ADD:
- The generated code config, when parsed via `crate::toml_simple::toml_load` (write it to a tempfile first, or if toml_load needs a path use a temp dir), yields `workspace.path == "/"`, `workspace.other-repo == ".claude"`, and NO `bot-session.col-width` / `push.state-file` keys (they're commented). If writing a tempfile is awkward in this test module, at minimum assert the generated string contains `"# col-width = 68"` and `"# state-file = \"push-state.toml\""` (commented) and that it does NOT contain an uncommented `col-width = 68` (i.e. `.contains("# col-width = 68")` true; and `!generated.lines().any(|l| l.trim_start().starts_with("col-width"))`).

# Validate (must all pass)
- `cargo fmt`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test`
Fix and re-run until green. Avoid `.unwrap()`/`.expect()` in non-test code (tests may use them).

Also run: `cargo run -- init --por /tmp/claude/vcx1-init-check-$$` (or any fresh temp path under /tmp/claude) and `cat` the generated `.vc-config.toml`, and separately generate a dual repo if easy; paste the generated code-config content in your report. Clean up the temp dir after.

Report: files changed, the generated `.vc-config.toml` content (code role and app-only role), confirmation the commented keys don't parse as active, and that fmt/clippy/test are green.

1	//! Single source of truth for every settable config key across
2	//! vc-x1's two config homes (the user config file and the
3	//! per-repo workspace config).
4	//!
5	//! - `crate::config_cmd` prints this registry as an annotated
6	//!   schema.
7	//! - Later commits derive init's commented defaults and a
8	//!   `--validate` unknown-key check from `schema()`, so those
9	//!   surfaces cannot drift from this list.
10	
11	/// Which config home a key belongs to.
12	/// - `User` — `~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml`
13	/// - `WorkspaceCode` / `WorkspaceBot` — `<root>/.vc-config.toml`
14	///   in the work repo vs the `.claude` bot repo
15	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
16	pub enum Home {
17	    User,
18	    WorkspaceCode,
19	    WorkspaceBot,
20	}
21	
22	/// The value shape a key expects (for rendering / future typed
23	/// use).
24	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
25	pub enum ValueKind {
26	    Str,
27	    Usize,
28	    ItemList,
29	}
30	
31	/// One settable configuration key.
32	pub struct ConfigKey {
33	    /// Dotted TOML path, e.g. `"bot-session.col-width"`.
34	    pub path: &'static str,
35	    /// Which home(s) accept this key.
36	    pub homes: &'static [Home],
37	    /// The value shape this key expects.
38	    pub kind: ValueKind,
39	    /// Rendered default, `None` if there is no default.
40	    pub default: Option<&'static str>,
41	    /// Active (not commented) in init; the value is role-specific.
42	    pub required: bool,
43	    /// The path has a `<placeholder>` segment matching a family
44	    /// of keys (e.g. `repo.category.<cat>`).
45	    #[allow(dead_code)]
46	    // OK: consumed by the `--validate` unknown-key check landing in 0.71.0-5
47	    pub dynamic: bool,
48	    /// One-line description.
49	    pub doc: &'static str,
50	}
51	
52	/// Homes accepted by every `bot-session.*` key: all three homes,
53	/// since the user config and both workspace configs may each
54	/// carry a default.
55	const BOT_SESSION_HOMES: &[Home] = &[Home::User, Home::WorkspaceCode, Home::WorkspaceBot];
56	
57	/// Homes accepted by workspace-only keys (`workspace.*`,
58	/// `push.*`): the two `.vc-config.toml` homes, never the user
59	/// config.
60	const WORKSPACE_HOMES: &[Home] = &[Home::WorkspaceCode, Home::WorkspaceBot];
61	
62	/// The complete registry of settable keys.
63	///
64	/// - User home: `default.*`, `repo.*`, and the per-account
65	///   `account.<name>.repo.*` family.
66	/// - `bot-session.*`: accepted in all three homes.
67	/// - Workspace-only: `workspace.*`, `push.*`.
68	const SCHEMA: &[ConfigKey] = &[
69	    ConfigKey {
70	        path: "default.account",
71	        homes: &[Home::User],
72	        kind: ValueKind::Str,
73	        default: None,
74	        required: false,
75	        dynamic: false,
76	        doc: "Default account when --account is absent",
77	    },
78	    ConfigKey {
79	        path: "default.debug",
80	        homes: &[Home::User],
81	        kind: ValueKind::Str,
82	        default: None,
83	        required: false,
84	        dynamic: false,
85	        doc: "Default --debug value when used without an argument (reserved; not yet consumed)",
86	    },
87	    ConfigKey {
88	        path: "repo.default",
89	        homes: &[Home::User],
90	        kind: ValueKind::Str,
91	        default: None,
92	        required: false,
93	        dynamic: false,
94	        doc: "Top-level shorthand: repo category to use when --repo is absent",
95	    },
96	    ConfigKey {
97	        path: "repo.category.<cat>",
98	        homes: &[Home::User],
99	        kind: ValueKind::Str,
100	        default: None,
101	        required: false,
102	        dynamic: true,
103	        doc: "Top-level shorthand: literal value for repo category <cat>",
104	    },
105	    ConfigKey {
106	        path: "account.<name>.repo.default",
107	        homes: &[Home::User],
108	        kind: ValueKind::Str,
109	        default: None,
110	        required: false,
111	        dynamic: true,
112	        doc: "Per-account default repo category",
113	    },
114	    ConfigKey {
115	        path: "account.<name>.repo.category.<cat>",
116	        homes: &[Home::User],
117	        kind: ValueKind::Str,
118	        default: None,
119	        required: false,
120	        dynamic: true,
121	        doc: "Per-account literal value for repo category <cat>",
122	    },
123	    ConfigKey {
124	        path: "bot-session.items",
125	        homes: BOT_SESSION_HOMES,
126	        kind: ValueKind::ItemList,
127	        default: Some("headers,user,assistant,tool,summary"),
128	        required: false,
129	        dynamic: false,
130	        doc: "Default bot-session item set (comma-separated)",
131	    },
132	    ConfigKey {
133	        path: "bot-session.result-lines",
134	        homes: BOT_SESSION_HOMES,
135	        kind: ValueKind::Usize,
136	        default: Some("10"),
137	        required: false,
138	        dynamic: false,
139	        doc: "Default --result-lines: max lines shown per tool result (0 = unlimited)",
140	    },
141	    ConfigKey {
142	        path: "bot-session.col-width",
143	        homes: BOT_SESSION_HOMES,
144	        kind: ValueKind::Usize,
145	        default: Some("68"),
146	        required: false,
147	        dynamic: false,
148	        doc: "Default --col-width: first-column width in the field-inventory views",
149	    },
150	    ConfigKey {
151	        path: "workspace.path",
152	        homes: WORKSPACE_HOMES,
153	        kind: ValueKind::Str,
154	        default: None,
155	        required: true,
156	        dynamic: false,
157	        doc: "This repo's path relative to the workspace root (role-specific: \"/\" for the work repo, \"/.claude\" for the bot repo)",
158	    },
159	    ConfigKey {
160	        path: "workspace.other-repo",
161	        homes: WORKSPACE_HOMES,
162	        kind: ValueKind::Str,
163	        default: None,
164	        required: false,
165	        dynamic: false,
166	        doc: "Relative path to the counterpart repo; presence signals dual-repo mode (role-specific: \".claude\" for the work repo, \"..\" for the bot repo)",
167	    },
168	    ConfigKey {
169	        path: "push.state-dir",
170	        homes: WORKSPACE_HOMES,
171	        kind: ValueKind::Str,
172	        default: Some(".vc-x1"),
173	        required: false,
174	        dynamic: false,
175	        doc: "Directory (relative to repo root) holding the push state file",
176	    },
177	    ConfigKey {
178	        path: "push.state-file",
179	        homes: WORKSPACE_HOMES,
180	        kind: ValueKind::Str,
181	        default: Some("push-state.toml"),
182	        required: false,
183	        dynamic: false,
184	        doc: "Filename of the push state file under push.state-dir",
185	    },
186	];
187	
188	/// Returns the complete registry of settable config keys.
189	pub fn schema() -> &'static [ConfigKey] {
190	    SCHEMA
191	}
192	
193	#[cfg(test)]
194	mod tests {
195	    use super::*;
196	
197	    /// Finds a schema entry by its dotted path.
198	    ///
199	    /// - Panics if not found: tests only, and a missing key is a
200	    ///   test bug worth failing loudly on.
201	    #[allow(clippy::unwrap_used, clippy::expect_used)]
202	    fn find(path: &str) -> &'static ConfigKey {
203	        schema()
204	            .iter()
205	            .find(|k| k.path == path)
206	            .unwrap_or_else(|| panic!("schema key {path:?} not found"))
207	    }
208	
209	    #[test]
210	    fn defaults_match_source_consts() {
211	        assert_eq!(
212	            find("bot-session.col-width").default,
213	            Some(crate::bot_session::COL_WIDTH.to_string()).as_deref()
214	        );
215	        assert_eq!(
216	            find("bot-session.result-lines").default,
217	            Some(crate::bot_session::RESULT_LINE_CAP.to_string()).as_deref()
218	        );
219	        assert_eq!(
220	            find("push.state-dir").default,
221	            Some(crate::push::DEFAULT_STATE_DIR)
222	        );
223	        assert_eq!(
224	            find("push.state-file").default,
225	            Some(crate::push::DEFAULT_STATE_FILE)
226	        );
227	    }
228	
229	    #[test]
230	    fn paths_unique() {
231	        let mut paths: Vec<&str> = schema().iter().map(|k| k.path).collect();
232	        paths.sort_unstable();
233	        let mut deduped = paths.clone();
234	        deduped.dedup();
235	        assert_eq!(paths.len(), deduped.len(), "duplicate path(s) in schema()");
236	    }
237	
238	    #[test]
239	    fn homes_non_empty() {
240	        for key in schema() {
241	            assert!(!key.homes.is_empty(), "{} has no homes", key.path);
242	        }
243	    }
244	
245	    #[test]
246	    fn dynamic_keys_have_placeholder() {
247	        for key in schema() {
248	            let has_placeholder = key.path.contains('<');
249	            assert_eq!(
250	                key.dynamic, has_placeholder,
251	                "{}: dynamic={} but placeholder-presence={}",
252	                key.path, key.dynamic, has_placeholder
253	            );
254	        }
255	    }
256	}

270	pub(crate) fn jj_chid(rev: &str, cwd: &Path) -> Result<String, Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
271	    let full = run(
272	        "jj",
273	        &[
274	            "log",
275	            "-r",
276	            rev,
277	            "--no-graph",
278	            "-T",

350	/// Write the POR (single-repo) canned `.vc-config.toml` into `dir`.
351	///
352	/// Split from the legacy `write_por_config` so the `.vc-config.toml`
353	/// write is gateable by `--config` while the `.gitignore` write
354	/// stays unconditional.
355	fn write_por_vc_config(dir: &Path) -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
356	    write_file(&dir.join(".vc-config.toml"), VC_CONFIG_APP_ONLY)
357	}

            instead of the URL-derived name.

Options:

          Account name — picks `[account.<a>]` from user config.

          output in one invocation.

      --repo <CAT[=VAL]>
          Repo target — `<cat>` or `<cat>=<val>`.
          
          - `<cat>` looks up the value via the account chain

          - `<cat>=<val>` uses the literal value, no config
            lookup needed.
